channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Late Night Tunes In to Operation Epic Fury: Live from Mar-a-Lago
item.description: The military operation’s name “is different from its original title, which was ‘Operation Epstein-o Distract-o,’” Jimmy Kimmel said.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Qué significa veracity
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 66 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: veracity
item.description: This word has appeared in 66 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: U.S. Attacks on Iran Test Fragile Truce With China
item.description: Beijing has condemned the U.S.-backed strikes on Iran, a close partner. Yet with trade talks looming, it is unlikely to risk a rupture with Washington.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, right, and the country’s top diplomat, Wang Yi, in Beijing last month.

item.title: ¿Por qué Trump decidió atacar Irán?
item.description: La decisión estadounidense de atacar Irán fue una victoria para Netanyahu, quien llevaba meses presionando a Trump sobre la necesidad de golpear lo que, según él, era un régimen debilitado.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Julian E. Barnes, Tyler Pager, Edward Wong, Eric Schmitt and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: As Trump Scrambles the World Order, Can Germany Learn the Language of Hard Power?
item.description: In the wake of the U.S. bombing of Iran and its dismissal of European allies, an anxious continent’s best chance at security runs through its largest economy.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Zerofsky

item.title: The Iranian Revolution That Wasn’t
item.description: Ayatollah Khomeini’s bait-and-switch game in the early years of the Islamic regime turned many of the people against it. They’ve been fighting the revolution since.
item.dc:creator: Abbas Milani

item.title: When Trump Meets Germany’s Merz, Iran Will Likely Dominate Discussion
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s visit to Washington, long planned, is now likely to be dominated by discussions of the attack, which Europeans did not take part in.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany, left, with President Trump in October.

item.title: U.S. Hunts for Militants as the Nigerian Military Is Accused of Inaction
item.description: The country has one of the most feared armed forces in Africa, yet within its own borders, it has repeatedly failed to stop abductions and attacks.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh, Ruth Maclean, Dickson Adama and Ismail Auwal

media:content.media:description: Razed homes are seen days after an attack that left dozens dead in the Muslim-majority village of Woro, Nigeria, last month.

item.title: In Myanmar, Junta Leader Eyes the Title of President
item.description: The military ruler of Myanmar, Senior Gen. Min Aung Hlaing, is expected to seek a civilian post to add a veneer of legitimacy after sham elections.
item.dc:creator: Richard C. Paddock
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat

media:content.media:description: Senior Gen. Min Aung Hlaing leaving a polling station in Naypyidaw, Myanmar, in December.

item.title: Global Economy Is Facing the Prospect of Another Profound Shock
item.description: A protracted conflict in the Middle East risks a spike in energy prices and broader inflation.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Goodman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Firefighters at the Ras Tanura oil refinery in Saudi Arabia on Monday, after a drone attack. The Middle East is the source of 30 percent of the world’s oil.

item.title: Corrections: March 3, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Tuesday, March 3, 2026.

item.title: Is a Pet Dog the Secret to Living Longer?
item.description: Research suggests dogs and other pets keep you healthier. Dana G. Smith, a reporter for Well, walks us through what exactly the science says about the connection between dogs and longevity.
item.dc:creator: Dana G. Smith and Theodore Tae

item.title: Quote of the Day: India Built the World’s Back Office. A.I. Is Starting to Shrink It.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, March 3, 2026.

item.title: ‘Pray We Make It Through the Night’: Iran’s Capital Under Siege
item.description: Many residents of Tehran managed to get out of town when the U.S. and Israel attack began, but others who could not described living under bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Arash Khamooshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Destruction of Cultural Antiquities and Heritage

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People are standing behind a yellow line after a police station on Niloufar street was hit by US-Israel attacks and damaged the next door buildings in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: At Eel Bar, a Party Where Toothpicks Were Crucial
item.description: The Basque-inspired Eel Bar hosted a pintxos-forward reunion for friends and fellow business owners.
item.dc:creator: Emilia Petrarca

media:content.media:credit: Lauren Lancaster for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Guests mingling during the cocktail hour.

item.title: OpenAI Amends A.I. Deal With the Pentagon
item.description: The new pact includes additional protections to prevent the use of the company’s technology for mass surveillance of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: “It’s critical to protect the civil liberties of Americans, and there was so much focus on this, that we wanted to make this point especially clear,” OpenAI’s chief executive, Sam Altman, center, wrote on social media.

item.title: Under Pressure from Trump, Cuban Leader Calls for ‘Urgent’ Economic Change
item.description: President Miguel Díaz-Canel said the private sector needed more autonomy, as the island confronts a U.S. oil blockade that has deepened a humanitarian crisis.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní and David C. Adams

media:content.media:description: President Miguel Díaz-Canel of Cuba, center, at a funeral in January for those killed in U.S. strikes on Venezuela.

item.title: Seven Takeaways From the Clintons’ Epstein Depositions
item.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton was defiant. Former President Bill Clinton spoke of President Trump’s ties to Epstein. A Republican raised a conspiracy theory.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni and Michael Gold

media:content.media:description: Hillary Clinton speaking to reporters after her closed-door deposition before the House Oversight Committee, as part of its investigation into Jeffrey Epstein, in Chappaqua, N.Y.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 3, 2026
item.description: Drew Schmenner gets a head start.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: Trump’s Head-on-a-Pike Foreign Policy
item.description: The Trump administration has deposed two sitting heads of state in two months, signaling a perilous new approach to the United States’ foreign affairs, argues the Times Opinion columnist Ezra Klein. “This is head on-a-pike foreign policy.”
item.dc:creator: ‘The Ezra Klein Show’
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Melania Trump Promotes ‘Peace Through Education’ at U.N. as War With Iran Expands
item.description: The first lady advocated an increased focus on education and empathy, as her husband has waged war in the Middle East and sought to cut education funding at home.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Melania Trump struck the gavel as she presided over a United Nations Security Council meeting, at the U.N. headquarters in New York City on Monday.

item.title: U.S. Embassy in Saudi Capital Hit by 2 Drones on Tuesday, Kingdom Says
item.description: Americans in Riyadh and other cities were advised to shelter in place immediately.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:credit: Brooks Kraft LLC/Corbis, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The United States Embassy in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.

item.title: Texas Senate Candidates Make Final Arguments Before Primary Elections
item.description: The Republicans John Cornyn and Ken Paxton — and the Democrats Jasmine Crockett and James Talarico — are battling in bitter and expensive races.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina

media:content.media:credit: Jordan Vonderhaar for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator John Cornyn greeted by supporters at a campaign event in Schertz, Texas, on Monday.

item.title: Can These Democrats Make Combative Centrism Happen for 2028 Election?
item.description: Third Way, the moderate Democratic advocacy group and think tank, has an expansive and expensive plan to shape the 2028 presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:description: Jon Cowan, left, president of the group Third Way, and Jim Messina, a Democratic strategist.

item.title: Trump Says He Will End Boycott of White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: The mentalist Oz Pearlman is this year’s speaker. “Hopefully, it will be something very Special,” the president wrote on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh and Michael M. Grynbaum

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Marine Corps Band playing at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington in 2024.

item.title: How President Trump’s Image Permeates the White House and Beyond
item.description: President Trump’s image — in paint and pixels, on posters and sculptures — is ubiquitous inside the White House, and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Doug Mills and Larry Buchanan

item.title: Trump Embraces U.S. Military Power After Years of Caution
item.description: The wars in Afghanistan and Iraq offered a stark lesson in the limits of military force. The Iran attacks suggest an era of postwar wariness is over.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump in Corpus Christi, Texas, last week. His sudden embrace of U.S. military power is a stunning reversal.

item.title: Trump Gambles His Presidency in His War With Iran
item.description: The risks for President Trump from the assault on Iran are escalating as casualties mount, oil prices rise and the war expands across the region.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump leaving the White House on Friday. Interventions in the Middle East have bedeviled generations of American presidents.

item.title: ¿Zendaya y Tom Holland se casaron en secreto?
item.description: El estilista de Zendaya, Law Roach, parece haber revelado casualmente que sí.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

media:content.media:description: ¿Marido y mujer?

item.title: Why Eliminating Iran’s Ballistic Missiles Could Be Difficult
item.description: Much of Tehran’s arsenal of missiles may be hidden underground, making it harder to destroy.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

item.title: Trump Administration Abandons Efforts to Impose Executive Orders on Law Firms
item.description: The move amounts to a surrender in a clash that has led many law firms to submit to the president rather than face the threat of his executive orders.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich and Michael S. Schmidt

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Justice Department told the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia on Monday that it was no longer interested in pursuing the cases contesting executive orders barring law firms from government business.

item.title: Supreme Court Sides With Religious Parents, Blocking California’s Trans Student Policy
item.description: Christian teachers and parents challenged the state’s policies, which they say require schools to hide students’ transgender status from their parents.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A protester holding a transgender flag outside the Supreme Court last month.

item.title: In First Appearance Since Taking the Country to War, Trump Is Business as Usual
item.description: President Trump gave a “brief update” on the Iran operation. And then he stayed true to form.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Donald J. Trump at the White House on Monday, his first appearance in public since the U.S. attacked Iran this weekend.

item.title: El gobierno de España niega a EE. UU. el uso de sus bases militares para el ataque a Irán
item.description: La nueva política es la crítica más reciente a Estados Unidos por parte de España, que ha destacado en Europa por su oposición constante al gobierno de Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: “No se usan y no se usarán las bases para nada que no esté dentro del convenio y para nada que no tenga encaje en la Carta de las Naciones Unidas”, dijo el ministro español de Asuntos Exteriores.

item.title: Trump Was Never Antiwar
item.description: Trump’s foreign policy has often been less a repudiation of neoconservatism than a mutation of it.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: What Rising Oil Prices Could Mean for Drivers in the U.S.
item.description: A prolonged conflict in the Middle East could cause a spike at American gas pumps, as the busy driving season approaches.
item.dc:creator: Emmett Lindner

media:content.media:description: A gas station in Chicago on Monday. The national average for a gallon of regular gasoline is less than $3.

item.title: In Republican Win, Supreme Court Retains G.O.P. District in New York
item.description: In an emergency ruling, the justices preserved the district of a Republican congresswoman, despite a lower-court ruling that it illegally diluted the power of minority voters.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle and Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Adam Gray for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A voter at a polling center on Staten Island in October.

item.title: The War in Iran: A Place for Student Questions and Reactions
item.description: How are you making sense of this news? Whether you already understand some of the context or whether it’s all new for you, we hope our free materials can help.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Schulten

media:content.media:description: The front page of The New York Times on Sunday.

item.title: Can Trump Legally Kill Iran’s Leader?
item.description: The United States and Israel started a war with Iran by killing its supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A woman holding a photo of Ayatollah Khamenei during a rally in Tehran last month.

item.title: U.S. Slaps Sanctions on Rwanda, Saying It Sabotaged Trump Peace Deal
item.description: Rwanda’s government responded by claiming the sanctions unjustly targeted only one party to the conflict and misrepresented the facts.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:description: President Trump, President Paul Kagame of Rwanda, center, and President Felix Tshisekedi of the Democratic Republic of Congo participate in a signing ceremony at the Institute of Peace in Washington in December 2025.

item.title: Trump Signals U.S. Is Prepared for a Long War Against Iran
item.description: President Trump also declined to rule out sending ground troops into Iran as the conflict spread into the Gulf countries and Lebanon and Israel exchanged strikes.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh, Tyler Pager, Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People gathering their belongings at their damaged homes on Monday after U.S.-Israeli airstrikes hit a police station in Tehran. President Trump said that still-bigger waves of airstrikes were coming.

item.title: Trump Foresees an Extended War
item.description: Also, how the U.S. decided to strike Iran. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Gandhi Hospital in Tehran today.

item.title: Limited Number of Flights Resume From Dubai Airports
item.description: A limited number of flights at Dubai International Airport resumed on Monday night after the airport shut down because of airstrikes in the region.
item.dc:creator: Axel Boada and Jackeline Luna

item.title: Rubio Warns Iran Faces ‘Hardest Hits’ Ahead, Offering Conflicting Rationales
item.description: The secretary of state signaled that an escalation in Iran was likely and said the attacks underway were both pre-emptive and defensive, describing a far more extensive mission than was initially laid out.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told reporters on Capitol Hill on Monday that the “next phase will be even more punishing on Iran than it is right now.”

item.title: Tuvo que llevar su violín en el regazo. Lufthansa cambió las normas para el equipaje de mano
item.description: En un vuelo a Alemania, Carolin Widmann tuvo que sostener su instrumento centenario, valorado en millones de dólares. Su situación resonó entre músicos que han enfrentado desafíos similares.
item.dc:creator: Christine Hauser

media:content.media:description: Carolin Widmann estaba abordando un avión con un violín Guadagnini, fabricado en 1782, cuando le indicaron que lo sacara de su estuche.

item.title: Texas Senate Contenders Make Closing Pitches on Eve of Primaries
item.description: The bruising intraparty races could have major implications for Democrats’ hopes of reclaiming the U.S. Senate in November.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Lexi Parra for The New York Times
